lines.text: Monica and Chandler's apartment, everyone except Ross is decorating the tree.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Let me ask you, why is everybody using these tiny lights nowadays? I remember when people used to use big lights.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That's a good story, Grandpa.
Ross enters

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hey

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow, Monica's letting other people help decorate her tree! Did someone get her drunk again or?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Having a perfectly decorated tree is not what Christmas is about. It's about being with the people that you love.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That is nice and we're done. TADA!
Her side of the tree looks a complete mess

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I dunno what it is, it just doesn't quite feel like Christmas to me.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, here.
She turns the tree around so that her side, which is perfectly decorated, is showing

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: See now it feels like Christmas!
Opening Credits
Central Perk, everyone except Phoebe and Joey are there.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: ... and that's the story of the dreidle. Now, some people trace the Christmas tree back to the Egyptians, who used to bring green palm branches into their huts on the shortest day of the year, symbolising life's triumph over death. And that was like 4000 years ago.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So, pretty much around the same time that you started telling this story.
Joey enters

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey you guys.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey Joe what's up?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I had to get out of the apartment. Janine is like stretching all over the place. Y'know, everywhere I look she's like... (He imitates her stretching)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I can see why that's hard to resist.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I like her so much!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Aw, I'm sorry sweetie that she doesn't feel the same way.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I know. And she's so sweet. I just wanna feed her grapes and brush her hair.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You are aware that she's not a monkey, right?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I am so over Janine. I mean, yeah, at first I thought she was hot, but now she's like OLD NEWS!
Janine enters

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Hey guys!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi Janine!

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: I just got a call to be a dancer on a television special for New Year's Eve. It's called some sort of Dick'n Rock'n Dickie Eve.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hold it! Are you talking about Dick Clark's New Year's Rocking Eve?

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Yeah, that's what I said.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God! We love that show! I mean Ross and I have been watching it since I can remember!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ah, you're still just a little fat girl inside aren't you? (He kisses her on the cheek)

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Well I'm gonna be on it this year. I'm gonna be one of the party people.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You're gonna be a party person! Those guys rock the most!

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Well they said I should bring someone. (To Joey) Do you wanna be my dance partner?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Totally! I would love to spend New Year's with you.

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Well actually they're taping tomorrow. I don't really understand why.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, well you see how it works is, the part with Dick Clark in Times Square is actually live, but they tape some of the party stuff ahead of time. Yeah, not a lot of people know that.

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Yeah well, do you guys wanna come too?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Are you serious?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We are there!
Rachel laughs

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What, what are you laughing at?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I used to date him, but you're still going out with her!
Gunther brings Janine a coffee

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Thanks. (To Monica and Ross) Great so we can all go together! I gotta run. Catch you later!
Janine leaves

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Bye!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Bye Janine!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Did she just ask me out on a date?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don't think so.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What are you talking about? She just invited him to the biggest party of the millennium!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, but she also invited you and Ross. Yeah, honey, I'm sorry, but I don't think that was a romantic thing.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh. Maybe. But hey I know how I can find out. We're going to a New Year's Eve party, right? So at midnight, I can kiss her. And if she kisses me back, great! Y'know? But if she says 'Dude, what the hell are you doing?' I can say 'It wasn't me, it was New Years!'

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, that's a lot better than Ross trying to kiss me in High School, and saying that he did it because he needed chapstick.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It was a dry day.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We are going to Dick Clark's New Year's Rocking Eve!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (To Rachel) Oh my God!
The Set, Joey, Janine, Ross and Monica are walking in.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: OK, listen I've been on sets before, so let me give you a little advice, alright? It's a show, but we're just dancing, OK? It's no big deal. The important thing to remember - stay cool.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Got it!
They get to the main stage

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh my God it's just like I dreamed it!

lines.speaker: Director
lines.text: OK, everyone gather up.
Monica and Ross push to the front

lines.speaker: Monica and Ross
lines.text: 'Scuse us

lines.speaker: Director
lines.text: Here's what's gonna happen. The music's gonna start, you're gonna dance, we're gonna tape, you don't look at the camera. Any questions?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, I have a question. When is this gonna air?
He and Monica laugh

lines.speaker: Director
lines.text: Uh, yeah. Now you guys dance over there, you guys over there, and I want you two right around here, and everyone else spread out.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ross! So when is it gonna air?
Monica and Chandler's apartment, Chandler is there. Phoebe and Rachel enter.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, you look in the kitchen, I will look in the back closet.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I can save you time ladies, I'm right here.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, Chandler why don't you take a walk? This doesn't concern you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: We are looking for our Christmas presents from Monica.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What? That's terrible!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No-no, we do it every year!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh well, that makes it not terrible.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, yeah, we never find them! She's always best at us that wily minx.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Don't worry, we're just gonna search here for an hour, them we're gonna go over to Joey's and search, OK?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No not okay, you can't look for Monica's presents!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But we have to!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, you don't have to, and you can't because I live here too.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well then, you should look with us.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Chandler, aren't you worried about what to get Monica for Christmas?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, I have a great idea for a present for her.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, that's it? (Mimics Chandler) A great idea!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Chandler, that's not enough. I mean what if she gets you a great present, two medium presents, and a bunch of little presents? And you've just gotten her one great present? I mean that's just gonna make her feel bad. Why would you do that to her Chandler? Why? Why?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: If I help, we can find 'em faster!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That's right!
Phoebe looks under the couch

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh ooh, we have a live one!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, it's a Macy's bag!
Phoebe tips it upside down, and a shoe falls out

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh, who's it for?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (Reading) Dear losers, do you really think I'd hide presents under the couch? P.S. Chandler, I knew they'd break you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh-oh. She may be onto us.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: We are so gonna find them this year.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know when you guys said you were gonna go across the hall and look, you don't, you don't do that every year do you?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You don't, like go into the back of my closet, and look under my gym bag or anything?

lines.speaker: Phoebe and Rachel
lines.text: No, we never do that
They turn away.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Because that's where Joey gave me some stuff to store that I've never seen before in my life! Okay, that did not just happen! (He does a weird clicky motion with his fingers, that kinda hard to describe.)
The Set, everyone is dancing.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, here comes another camera.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Right. No biggie. Stay loose.
As the camera passes by, they start to dance really rigid, but the camera is facing the other way.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Why do they keep doing that?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: If we wanna get on camera, I think we have to get up on one of those platforms. They've been taping those people up there all day.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Right.
They move towards a platform, dancing really strangely as they go

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, what'd you guys do to get up on there?

lines.speaker: Girl
lines.text: We learned how to dance.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh yeah? Well when you learned how to dance did you forget how to put on underpants?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah!
Camera pans to Joey and Janine

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Hey! You're a good dancer!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Yeah, well you'd be better if you just loosened your hips a little.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Like this.
She pulls Joey towards her and dances really close to him.

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: That's it, feel the rhythm. That's better.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh-huh.

lines.speaker: Director
lines.text: (To Joey) Okay, you're dancing with that girl over there.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No-no-no-no-no we came together!

lines.speaker: Director
lines.text: I don't see it. (To Janine) You are dancing with the tall guy over there. Tall guy, raise your hand! (He does. Janine goes over to him.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No-no hey buddy, please let me dance with that girl, I really like her and I think I have a shot.

lines.speaker: Director
lines.text: Really, y'think so? I don't..
He leaves, and this girl grabs Joey and starts dancing really wild.
Commercial Break
Monica and Chandler's apartment, Phoebe and Rachel are there and Chandler enters.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I couldn't find anything at Joey--Hey-hey, oh hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, we found them. There were in the guest room closet behind some coats.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, and you have nothing to worry about 'cos they're all crap!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Those are my gifts, I got them for you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ohhh. Thanks Chandler they're great!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well Chandler, what is this very weird, metal A Z thing?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Those are book ends! That's a great gift!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, okay, I'm sorry, thank you for my azzz.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ha!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Make sure you put all that stuff back in the closet, okay?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes, okay, oh, by the way, I just gotta say, I think it's really nice of you that even after you've moved, you still keep storing that stuff for Joey!
Chandler does the weird clicky thing again.
The Set, the girl is still dancing with Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey-hey dancer girl! Can I go to the bathroom? I just.. (The girl starts dancing really close to him, so he picks her up, twirls her round, and puts her against a platform) Here we go. (He walks away to find Monica and Ross doing a really out of place dance) Looking good Gellers!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: We know!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, see that snippy guy over there? He's the one who decides who gets up on the platform. We should go dance by him.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay.
They dance over to him.

lines.speaker: Director
lines.text: Okay, everybody hold!
Ross and Monica high-five

lines.speaker: Director
lines.text: Next on the platforms are... (He points to the right of Ross and Monica, so they dance over to where he's pointing to. He points away from them) ...you two! And... (He points the other way, and Ross and Monica follow his arm. Again he points away.) ...You two!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Excuse me, sir, would it help if I weren't wearing underpants?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Monica! (Pause) Would it?
The director shakes his head.
The Men's Bathroom, the tall guy is there as Joey enters.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! Tall guy! Hey, listen, I wanted to talk to you about that girl that you're dancing with.

lines.speaker: Tall Guy
lines.text: She's nice, huh? To think I almost brought my wife to this!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, yeah, okay. Uh, look buddy, I came with that girl, and I had this plan to kiss her at the new years countdown - I'm trying to win her over, so I was wondering if..

lines.speaker: Tall Guy
lines.text: No I don't think so.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh come on man, you can dance with my partner, she's real, uh, mellow!

lines.speaker: Tall Guy
lines.text: Look, are you dating this girl you came with?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, I was hoping after tonight that maybe I could you know...

lines.speaker: Tall Guy
lines.text: No, no. She's fair game if you ask me, sorry buddy!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Alright, alright, hey y'know fair is fair, (he pretends to wash his hands) if you're right, you're right, what can I say, but hey oh no! (He throws water on the guy's pants)

lines.speaker: Tall Guy
lines.text: God! What are you, in second grade?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, now you're the one who wet his pants. (He throws another handful on him and runs out)
The Set, Monica and Ross are dancing.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Man, this sucks! Y'know if Mom and Dad don't see us on TV after we made them so jealous, I mean, who's gonna be the losers then?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, I know what'll get us up on a platform!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: The routine!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ross, we haven't done the routine since middle school.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, when the snippy guy sees the routine, he'll wanna build us our own platform!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Was it really that good?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: We got honourable mention in the brother/sister dance category! Look, it's almost fake midnight, do we really have any other choice?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, let's do it. Mom and Dad are gonna be so faced!
They move into a space, and Ross points to his eyes and then to hers
They start the routine. However hard I try, I really cannot describe it. You will have to watch it. I'm sorry.
When they finish, they walk over to the director

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So, do we really have to ask who's going up on the platform next?

lines.speaker: Director
lines.text: Oh no! You get up there and do that again exactly like that!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes! (They run over to a platform)

lines.speaker: Director
lines.text: (To Cameraman) Make sure you get this, they're gonna want it for the bloopers show. (To everyone) Alright cut! Listen up everyone, when we start again it's gonna be the countdown to new years, so I wanna see everybody's excitement.
[Cut to Joey and Janine]

lines.speaker: Tall Guy
lines.text: Hey, pal, you have about three seconds to get away from my partner.

lines.speaker: Director
lines.text: What's going on over here?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, take a look at the guy's pants! I mean, I know you told us to show excitement, but don't you think he went a little overboard?

lines.speaker: Director
lines.text: What's the matter with you? Get out of here!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, take a hike wetpants!
The director pushes the Tall Guy away
[Cut to Ross and Monica, who are finally on a platform!]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Can you believe this? We're gonna be on the platform for the Millennium moment!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know! Hey, you haven't been practising the routine, have you?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Me too!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, when the music starts up again, I was thinking of maybe goind into the robot, y'know? (He mimics a robot)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ross, I think we should stick to the routine, we don't wanna look stupid!

lines.speaker: Director
lines.text: Alright we're back! Ten seconds left here we go! Ten! Nine! Eight! Seven! (He continues to count as we hear Joey think)

lines.speaker: Joey's Head
lines.text: Okay, it all comes down to this. Whatever happens, happens. Destiny.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Three, Two, One!

lines.speaker: Director
lines.text: Cut!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No! Year! Happy No Year!

lines.speaker: Director
lines.text: Okay, here's where we go to the live shot of Times Square, nice work everyone that's a wrap!
Monica and Chandler's apartment, Chandler and Rachel are there, and Phoebe enters with the Chick and the Duck.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey! Look who I found!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, hey guys!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Y'know, birds have a very good sense of direction, and I thought maybe they could help us find where the presents are hidden.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, if the presents are hidden south for the winter.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Or we could just follow your clever jokes - any ideas? No! Didn't think so! Okay, c'mon guys, show us where the presents are!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, the duck seems to think that Monica got me garbage! Hmm, I wonder what I could get Monica that's as good as garbage?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hmm, how about my azzz?
Rachel sits on the window seat, and knocks against it

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey, this is hollow.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: This bench, it's hollow! I can't believe I never knew that! (She pushes all the pillows off it and opens it up) Oh, the presents!!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, don't look directly at them!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Alright, no, we could look at them!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, this one's for me!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, this one's for Chandler. Here.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh great!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: And the big one's for me!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ooh, let's open them!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay! Okay! Okay!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hee hee!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wait, we can't do this.

lines.speaker: Phoebe and Rachel
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don't wanna know what Monica got me. Y'know? I mean, look, I'm sure she worked really hard at getting you a present, and wanting to surprise me, and you guys are gonna ruin that, and I, look we have to put these back, this is not what Christmas is about.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Whatever Linus, I'm opening mine.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Nobody is opening anything ok? Look, I don't know about you guys, but I wanna see the look on Monica's face when I give her my present, and I'm sure she wants to see the look on my face when I get mine. So please, please, can we just, can we put them back?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Will you get us better gifts?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Fine!
Monica enters, and they throw the gifts behind them.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey guys! You found the presents? Chandler, you let them find the presents? Great! Do you know how long it took me to find you that water purifier?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's what you got me?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh yes, I see what you mean. That look is priceless.
Rachel leans over to look at him.
Joey and Janine's apartment, they both enter.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Home sweet home, huh? Nice to, uh, get back to reality. Plus we know how the New Year's gonna go off. I guess there's no reason for all that Y2K panic, y'know? Anyway, g'night!

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: (She takes his hands) 3, 2, 1. (She kisses him) Happy New Year.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, yoii. What was that for?

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Well, I don't know tonight when they yelled cut and we didn't get to kiss, I was really, really, disappointed, and I just, really wanted to kiss you

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Really? In the moment, I really wanted to kiss you too. In the moment.

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: In the moment, yeah.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But only in the moment. So do ya wanna kiss again?

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Sure, New Year's Eve is only two weeks away. Can you wait?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Me neither.

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Joey, you don't have to count down every time we kiss.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, yeah, okay. Except I sorta felt like I needed a couple of seconds to get ready.
They kiss to the music of Auld Lang Syne
Ending Credits
Monica and Chandler's apartment, everyone except Joey is there.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We were on the platform, ready to dance the world into the new Millennium, and the guy yelled 'CUT!'

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh, wait, so you guys are telling me you actually did the routine from eighth grade?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah. But of course we had to update it a little bit. (To Ross) Hey, by the way, great thinking about catching me!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: 'Cos I was gonna say there's no way you could've done the end the way you guys did it back then!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What? We could do it!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don't know, I mean you were a lot bigger, I mean, stronger back then.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I can do it, okay? Come on, let's go. (She and Ross get up)

lines.speaker: Monica and Ross
lines.text: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8 (Ross runs to be caught by Monica, but she moves out of the way)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I can't do it! (Ross falls into Monica's room)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Now you do that, you're on TV.
End
